--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
title: "Draft.dev - Company Overview"
description: "Draft.dev is a content-powered growth marketing agency for developer tools and platforms."
last_updated: "2026-03-04"
source: "https://draft.dev/"
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Draft.dev - Content-Powered Growth Marketing for Developer Tools & Platforms

Draft.dev builds technical content engines that compound and resonate with developers, search engines, and LLMs. Founded in 2020 by former CTO Karl Hughes, the company combines strategic marketing with hands-on technical knowledge to create content that performs.

Draft.dev is trusted by 100+ tech companies including Docker, JetBrains, Sinch, Redpanda, Amadeus, Loft Labs, Bright Data, and Descope.

## Key Facts

- Founded: 2020
- Founder & CEO: Karl Hughes (former CTO)
- Headquarters: Chicago, United States
- Subject Matter Experts & Reviewers: 300+
- Clients: 100+
- Content Pieces Published: 3,000+
- Target Audience: 100% technical

## What Draft.dev Does

Draft.dev delivers a breadth of authentic developer content backed by real engineering expertise. The network of 300+ seasoned engineers produces technically accurate content that resonates with developer audiences, search and answer engines, and algorithms. Experienced marketers and editors ensure every piece is derived from strategic goals and drives results while maintaining the depth developers expect.

Draft.dev handles the full content lifecycle: planning, production, and promotion. From strategy, to production, to distribution within weeks.

## Who Draft.dev Serves

Draft.dev's clients are primarily developer tools companies, mostly Series A- and Series B-funded. These companies have funding and knowledge that content marketing works for their audience, and they want to scale production and ensure what they write resonates with developers.

Draft.dev also serves:

- [Marketers in tech companies](/for-marketers)
- [Developer Relations professionals](/for-dev-rels)

## Contact

- [Book a Discovery Call](/call)
- [Newsletter](/newsletter)
- [Website](https://draft.dev/)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
title: "Services Overview"
description: "Overview of all services Draft.dev provides to developer tools companies."
last_updated: "2026-03-04"
source: "https://draft.dev/"
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Services Overview

Draft.dev provides content-powered growth marketing services for developer tools and platforms. Services span three core areas: from zero to growth, content strategies for new product releases, and scaling output without scaling headcount.

## From Zero to Growth

For companies without a content team yet. Draft.dev brings the strategy, production, and distribution across many content types: blog posts, videos, social collateral, comparison pages, lead magnets, and more. Draft.dev drives the first wave of developer signups. You build product, they build your developer audience.

## Content Strategies for New Product Releases

Introducing a new product or feature means educating a market that does not know it exists yet. Draft.dev helps build awareness fast with a coordinated content strategy across blogs, video, social, comparison and feature pages, and lead magnets. All backed by competitive analysis and SEO/GEO insights.

- [How Draft.dev drives awareness](/drive-awareness)

## Scale Output Without Scaling Headcount

More channels, more content. Draft.dev integrates seamlessly with existing teams to expand content production across channels: on-site articles, guest posts, video content, newsletter campaigns, social distribution, and more. Draft.dev analyzes, refreshes, and reports on content performance.

- [Technical content examples for 40+ clients](/technical-content-examples)

## Subscription & Engagement Model

A Draft.dev subscription provides:

- Developer Content Strategy
- Content Creation & Review
- Lead Magnets
- Content Refreshes
- Ongoing Analysis and Reporting

Retainers and custom offers are available upon request. Three-month minimum engagement. Delivery starts after an initial planning period.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
title: "Content Strategy Services"
description: "How Draft.dev develops data-driven content strategies for developer tools companies."
last_updated: "2026-03-04"
source: "https://draft.dev/dev-content-strategy"
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Content Strategy Services

Draft.dev combines deep technical expertise with product research and proven SEO and AI optimization methodologies to create content strategies that put your solution in front of the right developers at the right time.

## What's Included

- **Industry & Competitor Analysis**: Draft.dev analyzes your industry, competitors, positioning, product features, target audience, and brand voice to ensure authentic output.
- **SEO & GEO Research**: Comprehensive SEO and GEO research, assessment of existing content, and study of marketing funnels.
- **Content Roadmap**: Delivery of a content strategy and roadmap tailored to your business.
- **Stakeholder Alignment**: Unifying DevRel, marketing, and product teams with a cohesive content strategy.
- **Content Audit**: Content audit and competitive analysis.
- **Editorial Calendar & Workflow Design**: Planning for consistent publishing cadence.
- **Content Templates & Style Guides**: Ensuring consistent quality.
- **Measurement Framework & Dashboards**: Tracking cost per lead and content effectiveness.
- **Monthly Analytics & Strategy Reviews**: Ongoing performance optimization.

## Timeline

Initial strategy development takes 2–4 weeks:

- Weeks 1–2: Discovery and research
- Weeks 3–4: Presentation and approval

Ongoing support is provided to ensure successful execution.

## Metrics Tracked

- Traffic to key content
- Lead magnet downloads
- Demo or trial signups from content
- Engagement via newsletters, shares, or backlinks
- Movement of content-informed leads down the funnel

Monthly analytics check-ins evaluate what's working and adjust the roadmap accordingly.

- [Learn more about content strategy services](/dev-content-strategy)
- [Developer marketing insights](/developer-marketing)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
title: "Content Types"
description: "All types of technical content Draft.dev creates for developer audiences."
last_updated: "2026-03-04"
source: "https://draft.dev/content-types"
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Content Types

Draft.dev creates a wide range of content focused on helping technical buyers evaluate, adopt, and grow with your product. The most effective developer marketing strategies combine multiple content types to address different stages of the developer journey. Tutorials and guides build awareness and trust, while comparisons and persuasive pieces drive consideration and decisions.

## Content Formats

- **Blog Posts & Tutorials**: Step-by-step explanations for accomplishing specific engineering tasks, including code samples and screenshots. [Learn more](/tutorials)
- **Comparison Pages**: Technical comparisons helping developers evaluate tools and make decisions.
- **Landing Pages & Onboarding Content**: Product-focused content for conversion.
- **Technical Product Guides & Use Case Deep-Dives**: In-depth exploration of product features and applications.
- **Thought Leadership Pieces**: Expert perspectives on industry trends and best practices.
- **Lead Magnets & Gated Assets**: Downloadable assets with landing page copy optimized for MQL/SQL conversion. [Learn more](/capture-leads)
- **Content Refreshes**: Updating existing content to maintain relevance and performance. [Learn more](/content-refreshes)
- **SEO-Optimized Pages**: Content optimized for search engine visibility.
- **Video Tutorials**: Video content for developer audiences. [Learn more](/video-tutorials)
- **Webinars**: Educational webinar content. [Learn more](/webinars)
- **Social Collateral**: Content variations for social media distribution. [Learn more about social collateral](/learn/announcing-social-collateral-from-draft-dev)
- **eBooks & Whitepapers**: Long-form gated content for lead generation. [Learn more](/learn/technical-ebooks)

## Content Tailoring

Content formats and tone are tailored based on your goals, audience, and funnel stage. Draft.dev focuses on content aimed at software developers, data engineers, technical leaders, and sysadmins.

- [See content type examples](/content-types)
- [See technical content examples](/technical-content-examples)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
title: "How Draft.dev Works - Process"
description: "Draft.dev's process from research and strategy through content production and promotion."
last_updated: "2026-03-04"
source: "https://draft.dev/"
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# How Draft.dev Works

Draft.dev plans, produces, publishes, and promotes first pieces within weeks.

## Step 1: Research & Strategy

Draft.dev gets to know your company and defines a plan. This includes:

- Analyzing industry, competitors, positioning, product features, and target audience
- Understanding brand and tone of voice for authentic output
- Conducting SEO and GEO research
- Assessing existing content
- Studying existing marketing funnel
- Delivering a content strategy and roadmap for feedback

## Step 2: Content Production

Draft.dev publishes first pieces within weeks. Content types include:

- Blog posts and tutorials
- Comparison pages
- Landing pages and onboarding content
- Technical product guides and use case deep-dives
- Thought leadership pieces
- Lead magnets and gated assets

To ensure fast turnaround times, Draft.dev uses proven AI workflows to help draft pieces. Each piece is reviewed by real developers and edited by professional technical editors to ensure accuracy, clarity, and value for the audience. Draft.dev can manage publication via all major CMSs.

## Step 3: Content Promotion

Draft.dev helps promote content on Reddit, LinkedIn, Twitter/X, and more. They create variations for third-party syndication and assist in publishing and advertising pieces in niche publications and newsletters read by the target audience.

- [Distributing content on social media](/distributing-content-on-social-media)

## Timeline

- First pieces published within 3 weeks of kickoff
- Weeks 1–2: Aligning on goals, messaging, and content roadmap
- After that: Consistent weekly publishing cadence (targeting 2x weekly)

## Client Involvement

Draft.dev is built to minimize internal lift. Clients typically need to:

- Attend a kickoff call
- Review and approve a quarterly roadmap
- Provide occasional feedback on draft content

Draft.dev handles the rest, including full CMS publishing, distribution, and social media asset creation.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
title: "Content Refreshes"
description: "Draft.dev's content refresh service to combat content decay and improve existing content performance."
last_updated: "2026-03-04"
source: "https://draft.dev/content-refreshes"
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Content Refreshes

Best-performing content from 2–3 years ago is likely underperforming today. Version numbers change, search algorithms evolve, competitors publish newer content, and technical details become outdated.

## Why Content Refreshes Matter

- Content decay affects nearly 60% of blog posts within 12–24 months of publication
- Most posts experience gradual ranking and traffic decline without updates
- Content refresh strategies consistently outperform new content creation
- Traffic increases of 200–500% for refreshed content
- Requires 60–80% less investment than new content creation

## How Draft.dev Identifies Content for Refreshing

Draft.dev analyzes Google Analytics and Search Console data to identify pages with declining traffic, lost rankings, or high bounce rates.

## Recommended Refresh Cadence

- Technical content: Reviewed every 12–24 months
- High-traffic pages: Checked more often if possible
- Fast-moving topics (frameworks, cloud services): Every 6–12 months

Draft.dev provides a refresh calendar based on content performance metrics and topic volatility.

- [Learn more about content refreshes](/content-refreshes)
- [Blog post: Refreshing blog posts](/learn/refreshing-blog-posts)

# Case Studies

Draft.dev has worked with hundreds of successful companies. Here are selected case studies demonstrating results.

## Sinch Mailgun

When internal engineering bandwidth was tight, Draft.dev delivered SME-written tutorials, testing content, and editorial QA—fueling engagement while saving weeks of internal time. Achieved 20–45% CTR.

- [Read the Sinch Mailgun case study](/case-studies/sinch-mailgun)

## Status Hero

Henry Poydar, Founder & CEO of Status Hero, shares how Draft.dev helped their technical content efforts.

- [Read the Status Hero case study](/case-studies/status-hero)

## Loft Labs

Rahul Patwardhan, Senior Director of Demand Generation at Loft Labs, shares their experience with Draft.dev as a competitive advantage for sourcing quality technical content.

- [Read the Loft Labs case study](/case-studies/loft-labs)

## Earthly

Adam Gordon Bell, Director of Developer Relations at Earthly, explains how Draft.dev supported their developer content strategy.

- [Read the Earthly case study](/case-studies/earthly)

## Notable Client Results

- fabric. Inc: Referral traffic and organic keyword rankings increased by 3x. One post hit Hacker News resulting in 5 demo requests in a single day.
- Loft Labs: Steadier flow of content, helped brand visibility, some posts became the most popular published.
- Snyk: Instrumental in scaling content program and building educational materials for developer security.
- Gusto: Accelerated developer-focused marketing efforts and built competitive advantage as a developer-friendly platform.
- Descope: Accelerated technical content output while extending bandwidth of developer relations team.
- Redpanda: Go-to for practical, well-written content that resonates with technical audiences.

# Drive Awareness

Draft.dev helps developer tools companies build awareness fast with coordinated content strategies. This is particularly important when introducing a new product or feature that requires educating a market that does not know it exists yet.

## Approach

- Coordinated content strategy across blogs, video, social, comparison and feature pages, and lead magnets
- Backed by competitive analysis and SEO/GEO insights
- Full-funnel technical content including landing pages, tutorials and demo apps, code samples, and social collateral

- [Learn more about driving awareness](/drive-awareness)

# Capture Leads

Draft.dev provides a lead collection system with downloadable assets and landing page copy optimized for MQL/SQL conversion. This includes:

- Lead magnets and gated assets
- Landing page copy
- Performance optimization through monthly analytics reviews tracking cost per lead and content effectiveness

# Build Trust

Developers are a community that values technical accuracy over marketing speak and makes decisions based on hands-on experience. Draft.dev helps build trust with developer audiences through:

- Content created by 300+ vetted engineer-writers
- Review by subject matter experts and editors
- AI-assisted research and drafts with human review for accuracy, clarity, and value
- Authentic, technically accurate content that resonates with developer audiences

- [Learn more about building trust](/build-trust)

# For Marketers

Draft.dev helps marketing teams in tech companies create authentic technical content that resonates with developers and drives business results. High-quality tech content is written and reviewed by experienced developers and edited by professional editors.

## How It Works for Marketers

1. **Discovery Call**: 30-minute introductory call to learn about your company and marketing strategy
2. **Content Plan**: Customized content plan based on budget, goals, and marketing strategy
3. **Content Delivery**: Tested and edited content ready to publish every 1–2 weeks

- [Learn more about services for marketers](/for-marketers)

title: "For Developer Relations"
description: "How Draft.dev supports DevRel teams with technical content creation."
last_updated: "2026-03-04"
source: "https://draft.dev/for-dev-rels"
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# For Developer Relations

Draft.dev supports Developer Relations teams by extending their bandwidth for technical content creation, allowing DevRel professionals to focus more on core product activities and community engagement.

Draft.dev designs content strategies that balance community value with business metrics, ensuring every piece of content serves both developers and the bottom line. The comprehensive strategy includes:

- Stakeholder alignment
- Content audit and competitive analysis
- Editorial calendar and workflow design
- Content templates and style guides
- Measurement framework and dashboards
- Monthly analytics and strategy reviews

- [Learn more about services for DevRel teams](/for-dev-rels)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Write for Draft.dev

Draft.dev works with a network of 300+ subject matter experts, all professional software developers and engineers. Writers create content that resonates with developer audiences while being technically accurate.

Draft.dev uses AI to speed up research and drafts, but every piece is reviewed by real developers and edited by professional technical editors.

- [Apply to write for Draft.dev](/write)
- [Open job listings on GitHub](https://github.com/draftdev/jobs)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
title: "About Draft.dev"
description: "Company background, mission, and team information for Draft.dev."
last_updated: "2026-03-04"
source: "https://draft.dev/about"
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# About Draft.dev

Draft.dev delivers a breadth of authentic developer content backed by real engineering expertise. Founded in 2020 by former CTO Karl Hughes, the company combines strategic marketing with hands-on technical knowledge to create content that performs.

The network of 300+ seasoned engineers produces technically accurate content that resonates with developer audiences, search and answer engines, and algorithms. Experienced marketers and editors ensure every piece is derived from strategic goals and drives results while maintaining the depth developers expect.

Draft.dev's work has generated hundreds of thousands of leads for clients across the developer tools industry.

- [Learn more about Draft.dev](/about)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
title: "Draft.dev vs AI-Generated Content"
description: "How Draft.dev's approach compares to purely AI-generated content."
last_updated: "2026-03-04"
source: "https://draft.dev/draft-dev-vs-ai-gen-content"
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Draft.dev vs AI-Generated Content

Draft.dev uses AI to speed up research and drafts as part of proven AI workflows, but every piece is reviewed by real developers and edited by professional technical editors to ensure accuracy, clarity, and value for the audience.

This human-in-the-loop approach ensures that content maintains the technical depth, accuracy, and authenticity that developer audiences expect—something that purely AI-generated content often lacks.

- [Learn more about Draft.dev vs AI-generated content](/draft-dev-vs-ai-gen-content)
- [How Draft.dev uses (and doesn't use) AI](/learn/how-we-re-using-and-not-using-ai-writing-at-draft-dev)

# Blog - Technical Writing

Articles covering technical writing best practices, style guides, documentation, and career guidance for technical writers.

## Writing Guides

- [Ultimate Guide to Technical Writing](/learn/ultimate-guide-to-technical-writing)
- [Technical Content Writing](/learn/technical-content-writing)
- [Technical Writing for Engineers](/learn/technical-writing-for-engineers)
- [How to Write Persuasive Technical Content](/learn/how-to-write-persuasive-technical-content)
- [How to Write Technical Comparisons](/learn/how-to-write-technical-comparisons)

- [How to Write Technical Thought Leadership](/learn/how-to-write-technical-thought-leadership)
- [Writing Technical Roundups](/learn/writing-technical-roundups)

- [Technical Tutorials](/learn/technical-tutorials)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
title: "FAQ"
description: "Frequently asked questions about working with Draft.dev."
last_updated: "2026-03-04"
source: "https://draft.dev/"
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Frequently Asked Questions

## What kind of content does Draft.dev actually produce?

Draft.dev creates a wide range of content focused on helping technical buyers evaluate, adopt, and grow with your product. This includes blog posts and tutorials, comparison pages, landing pages and onboarding content, technical product guides and use case deep-dives, thought leadership pieces, lead magnets and gated assets, content refreshes and SEO-optimized pages, and more. Content formats and tone are tailored based on goals, audience, and funnel stage.

## Who writes the content? Are they technical enough?

All content is created by Draft.dev's network of 300+ vetted engineer-writers and reviewed by subject matter experts and editors. Draft.dev also uses AI to speed up research and drafts, but every piece is reviewed by real developers and edited by professional technical editors to ensure accuracy, clarity, and value for the audience.

## How quickly can Draft.dev start delivering content?

First pieces are published within 3 weeks of kickoff. The first 1–2 weeks are spent aligning on goals, messaging, and the content roadmap. After that, Draft.dev targets a consistent weekly publishing cadence, with 2x weekly publishing as the first core discipline of a successful content operation.

## How much internal lift is required?

Draft.dev is built to minimize internal lift. Clients typically need to attend a kickoff call, review and approve a quarterly roadmap, and provide occasional feedback on draft content. Draft.dev handles the rest, including full CMS publishing, distribution, and social media asset creation.

## What is the minimum engagement?

Three-month minimum engagement. Delivery starts after an initial planning period. Retainers and custom offers are available upon request. Many clients start small as a test, adding in paid growth options over time.

## How does Draft.dev measure success?

Success depends on goals, but Draft.dev typically tracks traffic to key content, lead magnet downloads, demo or trial signups from content, engagement via newsletters, shares, or backlinks, and movement of content-informed leads down the funnel. Monthly analytics check-ins evaluate what's working and adjust the roadmap accordingly.
